book.title_en: A Glass Bridge Between Two Worlds
book.title_ja: ビーズの魔法：ムラノと京都をつなぐガラスの橋
book.author: Maurizio Lotter

silence_types.untranslatable_technique: embodied craft knowledge that cannot be fully encoded in language
silence_types.between_languages: structural gap between English and Japanese that constitutes a third field
silence_types.threatened_tradition: systemic fading of craft transmission across generations
silence_types.deferred_perception: field activation delayed between possession and recognition

editorial_principle: Silence is not absence but structural excess: what remains when language, technique, and transmission reach their limit. The bilingual form of the book is itself a device that exposes this irreducible remainder.

silences.label: sapere_incarnato
silences.basho: embodied technique exists only as enacted timing and pressure within the furnace field; it cannot be fully externalized as description without loss
silences.kokyu: knowledge is accumulated temporal pressure: repetition without abstraction produces mastery that resists linguistic capture
silences.yūgen: what is most refined in craft is precisely what disappears when one attempts to describe it fully

non_integrable_residue.description: embodied knowledge exceeds representational encoding by structural necessity

affects.silence-02: embodied excess generates the structural gap between languages

silences.textual_anchor_en: Within them, you will find techniques refined over centuries, along with the deep emotions and dedication of master artisans.
silences.textual_anchor_ja: ここには、何世紀にもわたって磨かれてきた技法と、名匠たちの深い情熱と献身が詰まっています。

silences.label: tra_le_lingue
silences.basho: between English and Japanese there is not translation but interference: a third field emerges that neither language can stabilize
silences.kokyu: reading rhythm splits into parallel flows that intermittently align without converging
silences.yūgen: beauty functions here as a shared limit of articulation rather than a shared expression

non_integrable_residue.description: the interlingual field cannot be resolved into a unified semantic system

silences.label: tradizione_minacciata
silences.basho: threatened tradition is a structural condition of modern craft systems: continuity is no longer guaranteed but actively maintained
silences.kokyu: transmission decays over time unless reactivated by practice; silence increases as generational links weaken
silences.yūgen: documentation records presence but cannot substitute for living practice; loss remains structurally possible

non_integrable_residue.description: absence of practice produces irreversible silence in the system

silences.textual_anchor_en: The Committee for the Safeguarding of the Art of Venetian Glass Beads
silences.textual_anchor_ja: ヴェネツィアンガラスビーズの芸術を保護する委員会

silences.label: perla_in_tasca
silences.label_ja: ポケットの中のビーズ
silences.basho: the bead in the pocket is a latent field: presence without perception, object without activation
silences.kokyu: time delay is required for perception to occur; immediacy blocks recognition, distance enables it
silences.yūgen: what is not seen at the moment of encounter becomes the origin of the entire narrative system

non_integrable_residue.description: latent perception cannot be reduced to missed attention; it is structurally generative

silences.textual_anchor_en: In the rush of the moment, I put them in my pocket without examining them as I should have.
silences.textual_anchor_ja: その時の混乱の中で、私はそれらをじっくり観察することなく、ただポケットにしまいました。

system_note.narrative_model: four structural silences: embodiment, interference, decay, delay
system_note.system_behavior: silence is redefined as structural surplus across language, time, and transmission rather than absence of meaning
system_note.compatibility: NarrativeObject v4.1 / NarrativeScene v4.1 / NarrativeSystemGraph v4.1
